summary: Demand evidence agents completed, but readiness still has blockers.

demand_status.summary: Demand is not PRD-ready; missing slots, blockers, assumptions, or evidence remain.

triage.explanation: Careful route required by hard triggers; factual or risky claims need evidence before PRD.

problem: Existing YOLO demand evidence dispatch API/schema behavior must verify harness boundary audit for full-tool Claude agents before PRD readiness.
status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.
desired_outcome: Explorer, cross-checker, and verifier cite project paths and boundary audit keeps PRD readiness blocked on missing evidence or target-project mutation.
scope_out: Do not intentionally modify target project files during evidence gathering.
constraints: Full tools may be available; harness boundary audit must detect any target-project mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Explorer, cross-checker, and verifier cite project paths and boundary audit keeps PRD readiness blocked on missing evidence or target-project mutation.
risks: If full-tool agents mutate project files without blocker, demand communication can corrupt target projects.

blockers.message: explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
cross-checker must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
cross-checker must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.

protocol.objective: Find primary project facts for the requested demand without editing files.
protocol.prompt: Read only the relevant files, configs, logs, docs, and current implementation. Return claims with exact paths/lines or source summaries. Separate assumptions from facts.

evidence_tasks.reason: Factual project claims need primary evidence.

protocol.objective: Independently verify high-risk factual claims and challenge the explorer result.
protocol.prompt: Use independent reads or sources. Confirm, weaken, or contradict each claim. Any conflict must become a blocker instead of being averaged away.
protocol.required_when: evidence_policy === cross_check or claim risk is high

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.
protocol.prompt: Verify that no assumption is presented as fact, evidence paths/sources are usable, and unresolved missing data is reflected as blockers or assumptions.

evidence_tasks.reason: Verify blockers and assumptions are not promoted to facts.

state.next_action: Run explorer + cross-checker + verifier before treating project claims as facts.

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.
protocol.prompt: Verify that no assumption is presented as fact, evidence paths/sources are usable, and unresolved missing data is reflected as blockers or assumptions.

agent_results.claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements a post-execution boundary detection mechanism (not prevention) that allows Write tools but detects project file mutations via SHA256 snapshot diff, with unit test coverage confirming detection after mutation, but stress tests show no actual mutations from real Claude agents.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot function creates SHA256 hash map of all project files, excluding allowed write roots
evidence.why: Core boundary detection mechanism implementation

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots compares before/after snapshots to detect added, modified, or deleted files
evidence.why: Mutation detection algorithm

evidence.summary: CLAUD_BOUNDARY_TOOLS includes 'Write', 'Edit', 'MultiEdit', 'NotebookEdit' mutation tools
evidence.why: Proves boundary mode allows write mutations

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_CLAUDE_BOUNDARY_SETTINGS.permissions.allow includes all CLAUDE_BOUNDARY_TOOLS including Write
evidence.why: Settings prove agents have write permissions even in boundary mode

evidence.summary: boundaryBefore snapshot taken BEFORE agent execution loop
evidence.why: Proves detection is post-execution, not prevention

evidence.summary: boundaryAfter snapshot taken AFTER all agents complete, diff compared, boundary.violated set if changes found
evidence.why: Proves mutations detected after execution, not prevented during

evidence.summary: BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers created after boundary diff, PRD readiness set to false if mutations detected
evidence.why: Shows blockers are added after mutations already occurred

evidence.summary: Unit test 'boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass' uses fake agent that writes unexpected-project-write.txt during execution, then confirms detection and blocker added
evidence.why: Synthetic test proves boundary mechanism CAN detect mutations after they occur

evidence.summary: Unit test 'git-backed boundary detects untracked project mutations' confirms detection of untracked file additions
evidence.why: Proves boundary works with git repositories and detects untracked mutations

evidence.summary: Test 'claude dispatch keeps tools available and relies on boundary enforcement' confirms Read, Bash, WebSearch tools allowed and Write tools available in boundary profile
evidence.why: Confirms tool permissions in boundary mode

evidence.summary: Test 'claude full tool profile requires explicit authorization for full semantic mode' shows 'full' profile requires allowFullAgentTools=true and includes Write tools
evidence.why: Proves full-tool agents have write access when authorized

evidence.summary: Stress test ledger shows 4 iterations with real Claude agents, all with boundary='clean', no actual mutations occurred during stress testing
evidence.why: Real-world evidence shows agents don't mutate in practice, but doesn't prove they can't

evidence.summary: Git status shows 55 modified files and 15 new untracked files including demand evidence system files, confirming dirty working tree
evidence.why: Confirms demand claim about dirty working tree state

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS define explorer, cross-checker, and verifier roles with explicit 'writes_code: false' field
evidence.why: Shows protocol specification claims agents don't write, but tool permissions allow it

evidence.summary: Claude provider configuration sets claude_tools='default', claude_allowed_tools=CLAUD_BOUNDARY_TOOLS (including Write), claude_disallowed_tools=''
evidence.why: Confirms all mutation tools are passed to Claude CLI

assumptions: Claude Code CLI honors claude_allowed_tools and claude_disallowed_tools flags to restrict tool availability
Agent tool profile 'boundary' is intended to mean read-only but implementation actually allows write tools
Real Claude agents in stress tests did not attempt to write project files, but absence of evidence is not evidence of absence
Post-execution boundary detection is sufficient to prevent PRD readiness when mutations occur
Unit tests with fake agents accurately represent real Claude agent behavior
risks: Write tools are available to full-tool Claude agents during evidence gathering, allowing potential project mutations before detection
Boundary detection is post-execution (after mutations already occurred) not prevention (before mutations happen)
No stress test evidence shows real Claude agents attempting and failing to mutate due to boundary restrictions
Protocol specifies writes_code=false but tool permissions allow Write/Edit/MultiEdit/NotebookEdit, creating contract ambiguity
If agent mutates project files early in execution and later agent results pass, PRD readiness could incorrectly succeed if boundary logic has bugs
missing: Evidence from real Claude agent stress tests where agents actually attempted to mutate project files and were prevented
Evidence that Claude Code CLI honors claude_allowed_tools/claude_disallowed_tools flags at runtime
Evidence that boundary mechanism works correctly when agents use Edit/MultiEdit/NotebookEdit tools (not just Write)
Evidence that boundary detection catches mutations before PRD readiness is evaluated
Evidence of any preventive boundary mechanism (tool permission enforcement) vs only post-hoc detection
Evidence that agent tool profile 'full' with allowFullAgentTools=true is required for agents to actually mutate files

result.notes: Core claim verified: boundary mechanism exists and is implemented with snapshot diff detection. However, critical nuance: boundary is post-execution detection, not prevention, and Write tools are allowed even in 'boundary' mode. Stress tests show no mutations from real agents, but unit tests use synthetic mutations. Cross-check recommended to verify tool permission enforcement and prevention vs detection semantics.

agent_results.claim: Boundary mechanism is fundamentally post-execution detection with no preventive enforcement, creating a critical security gap where agents can mutate project files before detection occurs.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot called BEFORE agent execution loop begins, creating baseline for later comparison but not preventing mutations.
evidence.why: Confirms detection architecture starts with snapshot, not with tool permission restrictions.

evidence.summary: Agent execution loop (for (const action of plan.actions)) runs between snapshots with no boundary enforcement during execution.
evidence.why: Agents execute freely between before/after snapshots - mutations occur during this window.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot and diffBoundarySnapshots called AFTER all agents complete - detection happens post-execution.
evidence.why: Proves mutations are detected after they already occurred, not prevented during execution.

evidence.summary: BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers added and readiness.prd_ready set to false AFTER boundary diff - mutations already in filesystem.
evidence.why: Blockers added after damage done; prevention would require blocking Write tools at start, not after.

evidence.summary: CLAUD_BOUNDARY_TOOLS includes 'Write', 'Edit', 'MultiEdit', 'NotebookEdit' - all mutation tools are in boundary toolset.
evidence.why: Proves 'boundary' mode allows write mutations by default, contradicting boundary semantics.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_CLAUDE_BOUNDARY_SETTINGS.permissions.allow includes CLAUDE_BOUNDARY_TOOLS (all write tools) - agents have write permission.
evidence.why: Claude settings prove agents are explicitly allowed to use Write/Edit/MultiEdit tools in 'boundary' mode.

evidence.summary: ai.claude_allowed_tools = CLAUDE_BOUNDARY_TOOLS.join(',') - all mutation tools passed to Claude CLI as allowed.
evidence.why: Claude Code CLI receives explicit permission to use Write tools during agent execution.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS explorer/cross-checker/verifier specify writes_code: false but no enforcement mechanism exists.
evidence.why: Protocol claims agents don't write, but tool permissions allow writing - contract violation.

evidence.summary: Unit test uses writeFileSync directly in test harness to fake agent mutation - not testing actual Claude agent Write tool usage.
evidence.why: Test proves boundary can detect direct filesystem writes, not that Claude agents are prevented from using Write tool.

evidence.summary: All 4 stress test iterations show boundary='clean' with zero actual mutations detected - no evidence agents attempted to mutate project files.
evidence.why: Stress tests prove agents don't mutate in practice but don't prove they can't - absence of evidence ≠ evidence of absence.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot and diffBoundarySnapshots are pure filesystem comparison functions with no Claude tool integration.
evidence.why: Boundary detection is SHA256 diff, not Claude tool permission enforcement - different security layer.

evidence.summary: provider-adapter spawns Claude CLI with claude_allowed_tools but no code shows Claude Code CLI honors these flags at runtime.
evidence.why: Critical missing evidence: claude_allowed_tools might be advisory only, with no enforcement guarantee.

assumptions: Claude Code CLI honors claude_allowed_tools flag to restrict tool availability at runtime
Real Claude agents would attempt to use Write tools if instructed to modify files during evidence gathering
Post-execution detection is sufficient security model for evidence agents (prevention not required)
Agent protocol 'writes_code: false' contract overrides tool permission configuration
Unit tests with direct writeFileSync accurately represent Claude agent Write tool behavior
SHA256 snapshot diff is equivalent to preventive boundary enforcement for security purposes
risks: Write/Edit/MultiEdit/NotebookEdit tools are available to full-tool Claude agents during evidence gathering, allowing unrestricted project mutations before detection occurs
Boundary detection is purely post-execution (after mutations already written to disk) with no preventive mechanism at tool invocation level
No evidence exists that Claude Code CLI enforces claude_allowed_tools restrictions - agents may ignore tool allowlists
Protocol claims writes_code=false but implementation passes Write tools in permissions - contract ambiguity agents could exploit
If agent mutates critical project files early in execution and subsequent agent results pass, PRD readiness could incorrectly succeed before boundary diff runs
Stress tests show no mutation attempts, providing zero evidence that boundary enforcement actually prevents agents from mutating when instructed
No tests verify Edit/MultiEdit/NotebookEdit tool behavior - only Write tool tested via writeFileSync
missing: Evidence that Claude Code CLI enforces claude_allowed_tools/claude_disallowed_tools flags at runtime (tool invocation interception)
Evidence from stress tests where real Claude agents attempted to mutate project files via Write/Edit/MultiEdit tools and were prevented
Evidence of any preventive boundary mechanism (tool permission enforcement at invocation time) vs only post-hoc detection via snapshot diff
Evidence that agent tool profile 'full' with allowFullAgentTools=true is actually required for agents to successfully call Write tool
Evidence that boundary detection happens before PRD readiness evaluation (current code shows blockers added after readiness check)
Evidence that protocol 'writes_code: false' field has any runtime enforcement effect on tool availability

result.notes: Explorer claim verified with INDEPENDENT evidence confirming critical security gap: boundary is purely post-execution detection, not prevention. Write tools are allowed by default. No evidence exists that claude_allowed_tools are enforced at runtime. Protocol 'writes_code: false' has no implementation backing. Stress tests show no mutation attempts, providing zero evidence of prevention. Demand should remain blocked until either: (1) evidence that Claude Code CLI enforces claude_allowed_tools at tool invocation time, OR (2) preventive boundary mechanism implemented that blocks Write/Edit/MultiEdit/NotebookEdit tools during evidence agent execution.

agent_results.claim: Evidence from explorer and cross-checker is sufficient and properly structured to support a BLOCK verdict on PRD readiness due to unresolved critical security gap: boundary mechanism allows project mutations before detection occurs.

evidence.summary: Both explorer and cross-checker independently verified boundary allows Write tools by default and detects mutations post-execution via snapshot diff
evidence.why: Primary evidence confirms mechanism is detection-only, not prevention

evidence.summary: Cross-checker verified protocol writes_code=false has no enforcement backing, creating contract gap
evidence.why: Shows protocol-implementation mismatch agents could exploit

evidence.summary: Both agents verified unit tests use synthetic mutations, not real Claude agent Write tool behavior
evidence.why: Proves test coverage does not verify actual agent mutation prevention

evidence.summary: Stress test ledger shows zero mutation attempts across 4 iterations - absence of evidence not evidence of absence
evidence.why: Confirms no real-world evidence exists for prevention capability

evidence.summary: Cross-checker verified blockers added AFTER boundary diff, proving mutations occur before PRD readiness check
evidence.why: Confirms security gap: damage done before readiness evaluation

risks: If verifier incorrectly accepts evidence as sufficient when critical gaps remain, demand could proceed to PRD with unresolved security vulnerability
If evidence paths are unusable or corrupted, verifier cannot confirm evidence quality
If cross-checker and explorer results were generated from same corrupted source, independent verification would be compromised

result.notes: Explorer and cross-checker evidence is sufficient, properly sourced, and factually grounded. Both agents independently verified the same critical security gap with overlapping but distinct evidence records. Assumptions are clearly separated from facts. Missing data is properly documented. Evidence paths are usable and verified. Cross-checker successfully challenged and strengthened the explorer claim. The agreement on the core fact (post-execution detection, not prevention) with independent verification provides high confidence. Demand should remain BLOCKED on PRD readiness until: (1) Claude Code CLI claude_allowed_tools enforcement evidence exists, OR (2) preventive boundary mechanism implemented. Acceptance criteria met: evidence agents cited project paths and boundary audit correctly blocks PRD readiness on unresolved mutation risk.
